title: Clippy: 14 unreadable long literals — insert digit separators
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Cognitive Readability
**Lint:** clippy::unreadable_literal

## Violation
Long integer/float literals are written without underscore separators (`1000000` instead of `1_000_000`). Rust's lexer accepts both; pedantic clippy flags the unreadable form.

## Locations
- src/compaction.rs: 1127, 1131, 1203, 1210, 1217
- src/hooks/mod.rs: 965, 973
- src/tui/app.rs: 1034 (three times), 1036 (twice), 1039 (twice)

## Mandated Refactor
Group digits every 3 from the right:
```rust
1_048_576   // not 1048576
200_000_000 // not 200000000
```
Auto-fixable via `cargo clippy --fix -- -W clippy::unreadable_literal`. tui/app.rs lines 1034-1039 are a cluster (likely pixel/dimension constants) — consider extracting named constants with UPPER_SNAKE_CASE instead of magic numbers inline.
